meta.notes: AI-generated proof-of-concept CPBS items for SpeedyCAT (chunk 5 of 10). NOT real MCAT/AAMC content and NOT from any answer key. Every stem and choice is plain text (no HTML/SVG/LaTeX). Answer keys were computed programmatically and each item passed a 4-criteria eval (four distinct choices; clean plain-text formatting; MCAT-appropriate difficulty; exactly one correct choice).

questions.stem: What ionic charge do elements in group 16 (oxygen group) most commonly form?
questions.explanation: Elements in group 16 (oxygen group) typically form ions with a charge of -2.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: How much power is delivered if 1200 J of work is done in 10 s?
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: Resistors of 47 Ω, 33 Ω are connected in series. What is the total resistance?
questions.explanation: Series resistances add: R_total = 47 + 33 = 80 Ω.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: What is the energy of a photon of wavelength 7 x 10^-7 m? (h = 6.63 x 10^-34 J·s, c = 3.0 x 10^8 m/s)
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: Overall, the molecule HCl is best described as
questions.explanation: HCl is a polar molecule because its dipoles do not cancel, giving a net dipole moment.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: What is the mass of 1 mol of CO2? (molar mass 44 g/mol)
questions.explanation: mass = moles x molar mass = 1 x 44 = 44 g.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: If 0.05 L of 1 M solution is diluted to 1 L, what is the new concentration?
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: How much heat is needed to raise 250 g of water by 1 degrees C? (c = 4.18 J/(g·°C))
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: An ideal fluid flows at 2 m/s through a pipe of cross-section 10 cm^2. What is its speed where the cross-section narrows to 2 cm^2?
questions.explanation: Continuity: A1 v1 = A2 v2, so v2 = (10)(2)/2 = 10 m/s.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: What is the maximum number of electrons that can occupy the n = 4 shell? (Use 2n^2.)
questions.explanation: Maximum electrons = 2n^2 = 2(4)^2 = 32.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: A surface has coefficient of friction 0.6 and normal force 200 N. What is the friction force?
questions.explanation: Friction f = μ N = (0.6)(200) = 120 N.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: Two resistors, 10 Ω and 2 Ω, are connected in parallel. What is the equivalent resistance?
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: Light travels through a medium of refractive index 1.6. What is its speed in that medium? (c = 3.0 x 10^8 m/s)
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: What is the strongest type of intermolecular force present in pure CO2?
questions.explanation: The strongest intermolecular force in CO2 is London dispersion forces.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: How many molecules are in 1.5 mol of a substance? (Avogadro's number = 6.022 x 10^23 /mol)
questions.explanation: molecules = moles x N_A = 1.5 x 6.022 x 10^23 = 9.03 x 10^23.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: A solution has pH = 5. What is its hydrogen ion concentration [H+]?
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: Adding 20900 J of heat to 250 g of water raises its temperature by how much? (c = 4.18 J/(g·°C))
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: An ideal fluid flows at 1 m/s through a pipe of cross-sectional area 10 m^2. What is the volume flow rate?
questions.explanation: Flow rate Q = A v = (10)(1) = 10 m^3/s.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: What is the maximum number of electrons in a f subshell?
questions.explanation: A f subshell holds at most 14 electrons.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: An object moves in a circle of radius 10 m at 8 m/s. What is its centripetal acceleration?
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: A 2 μF capacitor is charged to 5 V. How much charge does it store?
questions.explanation: Q = C V = (2 μF)(5 V) = 10 μC.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: An object is 12 cm from a thin converging lens of focal length 20 cm. Where is the image formed?
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: CO2 has polar bonds but is a nonpolar molecule because

choices.label: A
choices.text: carbon and oxygen have identical electronegativity.

choices.label: B
choices.text: it has lone pairs on the central carbon.

choices.label: C
choices.text: it contains only nonpolar covalent bonds.

choices.label: D
choices.text: its linear, symmetric shape makes the bond dipoles cancel.

questions.explanation: The two C=O dipoles point oppositely in the linear molecule and cancel, giving no net dipole.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: How many moles correspond to 1.81 x 10^24 molecules? (Avogadro's number = 6.022 x 10^23 /mol)
questions.explanation: moles = molecules / N_A = 1.81 x 10^24 / (6.022 x 10^23) = 3 mol.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: What is the pH of a solution with [H+] = 1 x 10^-5 M?
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: How many grams of water are heated if 4180 J raises the temperature by 5 degrees C? (c = 4.18 J/(g·°C))
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: Entropy is best described as a measure of

choices.label: B
choices.text: the total heat content of a system.

choices.label: C
choices.text: the disorder or number of accessible microstates of a system.

choices.label: D
choices.text: the pressure exerted by a gas.

questions.explanation: Entropy quantifies disorder/multiplicity; gases have higher entropy than liquids or solids.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: What is the atomic number of oxygen?
questions.explanation: The atomic number equals the number of protons; oxygen has 8.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: A spring with force constant 500 N/m is stretched 0.2 m. What restoring force does it exert?
questions.explanation: Hooke's law: F = k x = (500)(0.2) = 100 N.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: Two parallel plates 0.02 m apart have a potential difference of 12 V. What is the electric field between them?
questions.explanation: For parallel plates, E = V / d = 12 / 0.02 = 600 V/m.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: For a lens, the object distance is 10 cm and the image distance is 20 cm. What is the magnification?
questions.explanation: Magnification m = -di/do = -20/10 = -2.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: According to VSEPR theory, what is the molecular geometry of SO3?
questions.explanation: SO3 adopts a trigonal planar geometry based on its bonding and lone-pair electron domains.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: For the reaction 2 KClO3 → 2 KCl + 3 O2, how many moles of O2 form from 4 mol of KClO3 (with the other reactant in excess)?
questions.explanation: Use the mole ratio 3 O2 : 2 KClO3: 4 x 3/2 = 6 mol O2.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: At 25 degrees C, a solution has pH = 8. What is its pOH?
questions.explanation: pH + pOH = 14, so pOH = 14 - 8 = 6.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: How much heat is required to boil (vaporize) 30 g of water? (latent heat = 2260 J/g)
questions.explanation: q = m L = (30)(2260) = 67800 J for the phase change (no temperature change).
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: A system absorbs 250 J of heat and does 400 J of work on its surroundings. What is the change in its internal energy?
questions.explanation: First law: ΔU = Q - W = 250 - 400 = -150 J.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: What is the ground-state electron configuration of a neutral oxygen (Z=8) atom?
questions.explanation: Filling orbitals by the Aufbau principle gives oxygen: 1s2 2s2 2p4.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: How much work is done by a constant 15 N force pushing an object 10 m in the direction of the force?
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: How much energy is stored in a 10 μF capacitor charged to 10 V?
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: After 4 half-lives, what fraction of a radioactive sample remains?
questions.explanation: Each half-life halves the amount: fraction = (1/2)^4 = 0.0625.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: What is the approximate bond angle in SO3 (trigonal planar)?
questions.explanation: A trigonal planar molecule such as SO3 has bond angles of 120 degrees.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: What is the mass percent of C in CH4?
questions.explanation: mass percent = (mass of element / molar mass) x 100 = (12/16) x 100 = 75%.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: Using PV = nRT (R = 0.0821 L·atm/(mol·K)), what is the pressure of 2 mol of gas in 2 L at 273 K?
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: A reaction proceeds in two steps with ΔH1 = 150 kJ and ΔH2 = -150 kJ. If the overall reaction is the sum of these steps, what is the overall ΔH?
questions.explanation: By Hess's law, ΔH_overall = ΔH1 + ΔH2 = 150 + -150 = 0 kJ.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: A gas expands by 1 x 10^-3 m^3 against a constant pressure of 1.01 x 10^5 Pa. How much work does it do?
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: Which atom has the larger atomic radius, Mg or Ba?

choices.label: B
choices.text: atomic radius shows no periodic trend

questions.explanation: Ba has the larger atomic radius: same group; radius increases down a group, so Ba is larger.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: Newton's third law is best summarized by which statement?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Objects in motion tend to stay in motion.

choices.label: B
choices.text: For every force there is an equal and opposite reaction force.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Energy is conserved in an isolated system.

questions.explanation: Newton's third law: forces come in equal-magnitude, opposite-direction pairs acting on different bodies.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: How much work is required to move a charge of 5 C through a potential difference of 6 V?
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: A sample starts with 16 g of a radioisotope whose half-life is 20 days. How much remains after 20 days?
questions.explanation: Number of half-lives = 20/20 = 1; remaining = 16 x (1/2)^1 = 8 g.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: What is the hybridization of the central atom in H2O?
questions.explanation: The electron-domain geometry of H2O corresponds to sp3 hybridization of the central atom.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: A sample has mass 12 g and volume 90 mL. What is its density?
questions.explanation: density = mass / volume = 12 / 90 = 0.133 g/mL.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: A gas at 3 atm occupies 2 L. At constant temperature, what volume does it occupy at 0.5 atm?
questions.explanation: Boyle's law: P1 V1 = P2 V2, so V2 = (3)(2)/0.5 = 12 L.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: For a reaction, ΔH = -50 kJ and ΔS = 0.2 kJ/K. What is ΔG at 500 K?
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: A heat engine absorbs 2000 J and exhausts 300 J. What is its efficiency?
questions.explanation: Efficiency = 1 - Qc/Qh = 1 - 300/2000 = 85%.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: Which atom has the higher first ionization energy, C or Ne?

choices.label: D
choices.text: Ionization energy shows no periodic trend

questions.explanation: Ne has the higher first ionization energy: same period; ionization energy increases left to right, so Ne is higher.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: An object starts at 0 m/s and accelerates uniformly at 2 m/s^2 for 6 s. What is its final velocity?
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: Ohm's law states that

choices.label: B
choices.text: voltage equals current times resistance (V = I R).

questions.explanation: Ohm's law relates the three as V = I R for an ohmic resistor.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: The loudness of a sound is most directly related to a wave's
questions.explanation: Greater amplitude carries more energy and is perceived as louder; frequency sets pitch.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: How many valence electrons does a neutral fluorine (F) atom have?
questions.explanation: fluorine (F) is in a main group whose number of valence electrons is 7.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: Convert 4.2 kg to grams.
questions.explanation: Apply the SI prefix conversion: 4.2 kg = 4200 g.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: A gas occupies 4 L at 300 K. At constant pressure, what volume does it occupy at 600 K?
questions.explanation: Charles's law: V1/T1 = V2/T2, so V2 = 4 x 600/300 = 8 L.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: What is the equilibrium constant expression K for the reaction CO + 2 H2 ⇌ CH3OH?
questions.explanation: K places products over reactants, each concentration raised to its coefficient: K = [CH3OH] / ([CO][H2]^2).
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: Convert -40 degrees C to kelvin. (Use K = C + 273.)
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: Down a group (top to bottom), atomic radius generally
questions.explanation: Additional electron shells make atoms larger down a group.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: An object with initial velocity 8 m/s accelerates at 6 m/s^2 for 2 s. How far does it travel?
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: A current of 4 A flows through a 12 Ω resistor. What is the voltage across it?
questions.explanation: Ohm's law: V = I R = (4)(12) = 48 V.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: A wave has frequency 60 Hz and wavelength 5 m. What is its wave speed?
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: The bond in O2 has an electronegativity difference of about 0. It is best classified as
questions.explanation: An electronegativity difference near 0 indicates a nonpolar covalent bond (roughly: 0 nonpolar covalent, up to about 1.7 polar covalent, larger values ionic).
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: Which is a hallmark of a combustion reaction?

choices.label: A
choices.text: A single compound splits with no oxygen.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Two ionic compounds swap ions in solution.

choices.label: C
choices.text: A metal displaces hydrogen from an acid.

choices.label: D
choices.text: A fuel reacts with oxygen to release energy, often producing CO2 and H2O.

questions.explanation: Combustion is rapid reaction of a fuel with O2, typically yielding CO2 and water plus heat.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: What volume does 2 mol of an ideal gas occupy at STP? (molar volume = 22.4 L/mol)
questions.explanation: V = moles x 22.4 L/mol = 2 x 22.4 = 44.8 L.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: According to Le Chatelier's principle, increasing the temperature of an endothermic a system at equilibrium generally

choices.label: A
choices.text: shifts the equilibrium toward the products.

choices.label: B
choices.text: shifts toward the side with fewer moles of gas.

choices.label: C
choices.text: shifts the equilibrium toward the reactants.

choices.label: D
choices.text: causes no shift in the position of equilibrium.

questions.explanation: The system opposes the change: increasing the temperature of an endothermic a system at equilibrium shifts the equilibrium toward the products.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: Convert 250 K to degrees Celsius. (Use C = K - 273.)
questions.explanation: C = 250 - 273 = -23 degrees C.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: How many neutrons are in an atom of chlorine-35? (atomic number 17)
questions.explanation: neutrons = mass number - atomic number = 35 - 17 = 18.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: Overall, the molecule HF is best described as
questions.explanation: HF is a polar molecule because its dipoles do not cancel, giving a net dipole moment.

questions.stem: What is the approximate molar mass of CH4? (Use H=1, C=12, N=14, O=16, Na=23, Cl=35.5, S=32, K=39, Ca=40, Mg=24, Fe=56, Ag=108)
questions.explanation: Summing the atomic masses in CH4 gives a molar mass of about 16 g/mol.

questions.stem: A solution with pH = 3 is
questions.explanation: pH below 7 is acidic, pH above 7 basic, and pH 7 neutral (at 25 degrees C).
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: At chemical equilibrium,

choices.label: A
choices.text: the forward and reverse reaction rates are equal.

choices.label: B
choices.text: all reactants are converted to products.

choices.label: C
choices.text: the concentrations of reactants and products are always equal.

questions.explanation: Equilibrium is dynamic: forward and reverse rates match, so concentrations stay constant.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: What is the density of an object with mass 36 g and volume 400 cm^3?
questions.explanation: density = mass / volume = 36 / 400 = 0.09 g/cm^3.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: How many protons are in an atom of chlorine-35?
questions.explanation: The number of protons equals the atomic number; for chlorine-35 that is 17.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: What is the strongest type of intermolecular force present in pure Cl2?
questions.explanation: The strongest intermolecular force in Cl2 is London dispersion forces.
questions.sourceLicense: AI-generated proof-of-concept — not real MCAT/AAMC content

questions.stem: How many moles are in 34 g of NH3? (molar mass 17 g/mol)
questions.explanation: moles = mass / molar mass = 34 / 17 = 2 mol.

questions.stem: What is the molarity of a solution with 0.5 mol of solute in 4 L of solution?
questions.explanation: Molarity M = moles / liters = 0.5 / 4 = 0.125 M.

questions.stem: How much heat is needed to raise 300 g of water by 15 degrees C? (c = 4.18 J/(g·°C))

questions.stem: A force of 50 N is applied over an area of 2 m^2. What is the pressure?
questions.explanation: Pressure P = F / A = 50 / 2 = 25 Pa.

questions.stem: What is the mass number of chlorine-35?
questions.explanation: The mass number counts protons plus neutrons; for chlorine-35 it is 35.

questions.stem: An object is dropped from rest from a height of 45 m. How long does it take to reach the ground? (g = 9.8 m/s^2)

questions.stem: A periodic wave has a frequency of 5 Hz. What is its period?
questions.explanation: Period T = 1 / f = 1 / 5 = 0.2 s.

questions.stem: Compared with a single bond between the same two atoms, a triple bond is
questions.explanation: More shared pairs pull atoms closer, so triple bonds are shorter than double bonds, which are shorter than single bonds; the triple bond is also the strongest.

questions.explanation: mass = moles x molar mass = 2 x 40 = 80 g.

questions.stem: How many electrons are in the ion Al3+? (atomic number 13)
questions.explanation: Adjust the neutral atom's 13 electrons for the charge: Al3+ has 10 electrons.

questions.stem: What net force is required to accelerate a 10 kg object at 2 m/s^2?
questions.explanation: Newton's second law: F = m a = (10)(2) = 20 N.
